Container.Name: Izanagi
Container.Overview: Izanagi is a self-hosted Unraid template deployment service. It lets coding agents (Claude Code, Aider, etc.) push Unraid Community Applications XML templates and trigger container restarts via a simple authenticated REST API, with a built-in web UI for token management, deploy history, and more.

Config.Name: Bootstrap Token
Config.Description: Secret used to create your first API token via the UI. Remove after bootstrapping.

Config.Name: Templates Path
Config.Description: Path inside the container where Unraid XML templates are written.

Config.Name: Database Path
Config.Description: Path inside the container for the SQLite database file.

Config.Name: Port
Config.Description: Port the Izanagi web server listens on.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port exposed on the Unraid host for the Izanagi web interface.

Config.Name: Templates Directory
Config.Description: Host directory containing your Unraid Community Applications XML templates. This should point to your actual Unraid custom templates folder.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the Izanagi SQLite database (tokens, history).

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Docker socket for triggering container restarts from the API.
